title: What A.I. Kant Do
description: Could we English majors have a future after all?
author: Maureen Dowd

title: Trump Has Done More Than Misjudge Iran
description: The war has revealed the true puniness of Trump’s power
author: Lydia Polgreen

title: Catastrophe Is Emerging in the World’s Most Vulnerable Places
description: The humanitarian relief system, decimated by cuts, faces a grave challenge as the Middle East war causes soaring costs for food, fuel and fertilizer.
author: Peter S. Goodman and Finbarr O’Reilly

title: A Crack in the Polling Floor Puts Trump in New Territory
description: A 37 percent approval rating in a new Times/Siena poll suggests the G.O.P. is facing a big midterm problem despite recent redistricting gains.
author: Nate Cohn

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

title: In Closed-Door Talks, U.S. Demands a Major Role in Greenland
description: Greenlandic officials worry about the direction of the negotiations aimed at defusing President Trump’s threats to seize their island. But they have little leverage.
author: Jeffrey Gettleman, Maya Tekeli, Anton Troianovski and Eric Schmitt

title: Actually, Democracy Dies in H.R.
description: New research sheds light on how mediocre employees help would-be authoritarians maintain power.
author: Amanda Taub

title: We Need More ‘Rabid Partisans’ Like Shane Massey
description: Defeating a sclerotic rump of a party is no achievement. Instead, one-party rule enables corruption. It fosters stagnation.
author: David French

title: W.H.O. Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
description: The agency made the declaration just a day after Africa’s top public health agency first announced the outbreak. There is no approved vaccine for this species of Ebola.
author: Yan Zhuang, Matthew Mpoke Bigg and Apoorva Mandavilli

title: The Iran War Is Crippling One of the World’s Wealthiest Nations
description: Iranian attacks and the stoppage of seaborne transit have paralyzed Qatar’s vital gas exports, stalling the economic pivots intended to anchor the country’s growth.
author: River Akira Davis

title: In Iraqi Desert, Two Israeli Outposts Were Kept Secret for Months
description: Israel spent over a year preparing a covert site in Iraq for its operations against Iran, regional officials say. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.
author: Erika Solomon and Falih Hassan

title: Trump Administration Pushes Narrative of Christian Nation at Prayer Rally in D.C.
description: The day-long prayer event featured speakers from President Trump’s cabinet and a program that drew connections between the nation’s founding and Christianity.
author: Ruth Graham and Elizabeth Dias

title: Mamdani’s Nakba Day Social Media Post Marks Shift for N.Y.C. Mayors
description: Pro-Palestinian New Yorkers celebrated Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s decision to post about the Palestinians’ day of remembrance, but the move was criticized by some Jewish leaders.
author: Liam Stack, Dana Rubinstein and Sally Goldenberg

title: Cassidy Loses Senate Primary in Louisiana, as Trump Vanquishes G.O.P. Foe
description: Senator Bill Cassidy, a two-term Republican who voted to convict President Trump in his 2021 impeachment trial,  took a veiled swipe at Mr. Trump, saying power should not focus on “one individual.”
author: Michael Gold

title: How the Reputation Firm Terakeet Failed to Fix an Epstein Friend’s Image
description: Terakeet, a reputation management firm, used online tricks to downplay the friendship of the Goldman Sachs general counsel, Kathryn Ruemmler, with the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. It wasn’t enough.
author: Robert Draper

title: Want to See the Epstein Files in Print? Here Are the 3,437 Volumes.
description: Documents related to Jeffrey Epstein, taking up more than 3,400 volumes and weighing over eight tons, have been printed and bound for display in a TriBeCa gallery.
author: Jesse McKinley

title: To Critics, Trump Remarks Reveal a Billionaire Out of Touch
description: The president has never pretended to be an ordinary American, but a recent “truth bomb” has opened him to criticism that he doesn’t grasp the economic strain of his war with Iran.
author: Erica L. Green

title: Why Neutral Maps Could Empower Black Voters as Much as the Voting Rights Act
description: Our simulations debunk the notion that the act created artificial minority districts that wouldn’t otherwise exist.
author: Nate Cohn and Eve Washington

title: Nothing Beats Polarization Like Civics Education
description: The decline of civic education hit bottom about a decade ago and is at last on the rebound. This fact brings me hope.
author: Danielle Allen

title: Demon Attacks. Teleportation. Astrology. The Supernatural Is Back.
description: One of the oldest and most durable features of human experience is re-emerging.
author: Katya Ungerman
